name: cachelib

metadata.name: cachelib
metadata.summary: A collection of cache libraries in the same API interface.

documentation:
# CacheLib

A collection of cache libraries in the same API interface. Extracted
from Werkzeug.

## Installing

Install and update using [pip]:

```text
$ pip install -U cachelib
```

## Donate

The Pallets organization develops and supports Flask and the libraries
it uses. In order to grow the community of contributors and users, and
allow the maintainers to devote more time to the projects, [please
donate today][please donate today].

## Links

- Documentation: <https://cachelib.readthedocs.io/>
- Changes: <https://cachelib.readthedocs.io/changes/>
- PyPI Releases: <https://pypi.org/project/cachelib/>
- Source Code: <https://github.com/pallets/cachelib/>
- Issue Tracker: <https://github.com/pallets/cachelib/issues/>
- Twitter: <https://twitter.com/PalletsTeam>
- Chat: <https://discord.gg/pallets>

[pip]: https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/getting-started/
[please donate today]: https://palletsprojects.com/donate

classes.name: cachelib.uwsgi.UWSGICache
documentation:
Implements the cache using uWSGI's caching framework.

:::{note}
This class cannot be used when running under PyPy, because the uWSGI
API implementation for PyPy is lacking the needed functionality.
:::

```{eval-rst}

:param default_timeout: The default timeout in seconds.
:param cache: The name of the caching instance to connect to, for
    example: mycache@localhost:3031, defaults to an empty string, which
    means uWSGI will cache in the local instance. If the cache is in the
    same instance as the werkzeug app, you only have to provide the name of
    the cache.
```

classes.name: cachelib.serializers.BaseSerializer
documentation:
This is the base interface for all default serializers.

BaseSerializer.load and BaseSerializer.dump will
default to pickle.load and pickle.dump. This is currently
used only by FileSystemCache which dumps/loads to/from a file stream.

classes.name: cachelib.serializers.UWSGISerializer
documentation: Default serializer for UWSGICache.

classes.name: cachelib.serializers.SimpleSerializer
documentation: Default serializer for SimpleCache.

classes.name: cachelib.serializers.FileSystemSerializer
documentation: Default serializer for FileSystemCache.

classes.name: cachelib.serializers.RedisSerializer

methods.name: cachelib.serializers.RedisSerializer.dumps
documentation:
Dumps an object into a string for redis. By default it serializes
integers as regular string and pickle dumps everything else.

methods.name: cachelib.serializers.RedisSerializer.loads
documentation:
The reversal of {meth}`dump_object`. This might be called with
None.
Default serializer for RedisCache.

classes.name: cachelib.simple.SimpleCache
documentation:
Simple memory cache for single process environments.  This class exists
mainly for the development server and is not 100% thread safe.  It tries
to use as many atomic operations as possible and no locks for simplicity
but it could happen under heavy load that keys are added multiple times.

```{eval-rst}

it starts deleting some.
:param default_timeout: the default timeout that is used if no timeout is
                        specified on :meth:`~BaseCache.set`. A timeout of
                        0 indicates that the cache never expires.
```

classes.name: cachelib.redis.RedisCache

methods.name: cachelib.redis.RedisCache._normalize_timeout
documentation:
Normalize timeout by setting it to default of 300 if
not defined (None) or -1 if explicitly set to zero.

:param timeout: timeout to normalize.

Uses the Redis key-value store as a cache backend.

The first argument can be either a string denoting address of the Redis
server or an object resembling an instance of a redis.Redis class.

Note: Python Redis API already takes care of encoding unicode strings on
the fly.

```{eval-rst}

compatible with the official Python Redis client (redis-py).
:param port: port number on which Redis server listens for connections.
:param password: password authentication for the Redis server.
:param db: db (zero-based numeric index) on Redis Server to connect.
:param default_timeout: the default timeout that is used if no timeout is
                        specified on :meth:`~BaseCache.set`. A timeout of
                        0 indicates that the cache never expires.
:param key_prefix: A prefix that should be added to all keys.
```

Any additional keyword arguments will be passed to `redis.Redis`.

classes.name: cachelib.base.BaseCache

methods.name: cachelib.base.BaseCache.get
documentation:
Look up key in the cache and return the value for it.

```{eval-rst}

:param key: the key to be looked up.
:returns: The value if it exists and is readable, else ``None``.
```

methods.name: cachelib.base.BaseCache.delete
documentation:
Delete `key` from the cache.

:param key: the key to delete.
:returns: Whether the key existed and has been deleted.
:rtype: boolean

methods.name: cachelib.base.BaseCache.get_many
documentation:
Returns a list of values for the given keys.
For each key an item in the list is created:

```
foo, bar = cache.get_many("foo", "bar")
```

Has the same error handling as {meth}`get`.

```{eval-rst}

arguments.
```

methods.name: cachelib.base.BaseCache.get_dict
documentation:
Like {meth}`get_many` but return a dict:

```
d = cache.get_dict("foo", "bar")
foo = d["foo"]
bar = d["bar"]
```

```{eval-rst}

arguments.
```

methods.name: cachelib.base.BaseCache.set
documentation:
Add a new key/value to the cache (overwrites value, if key already
exists in the cache).

```{eval-rst}

:param key: the key to set
:param value: the value for the key
:param timeout: the cache timeout for the key in seconds (if not
                specified, it uses the default timeout). A timeout of
                0 indicates that the cache never expires.
:returns: ``True`` if key has been updated, ``False`` for backend
          errors. Pickling errors, however, will raise a subclass of
          ``pickle.PickleError``.
:rtype: boolean
```

methods.name: cachelib.base.BaseCache.add
documentation:
Works like {meth}`set` but does not overwrite the values of already
existing keys.

```{eval-rst}

:param key: the key to set
:param value: the value for the key
:param timeout: the cache timeout for the key in seconds (if not
                specified, it uses the default timeout). A timeout of
                0 indicates that the cache never expires.
:returns: Same as :meth:`set`, but also ``False`` for already
          existing keys.
:rtype: boolean
```

methods.name: cachelib.base.BaseCache.set_many
documentation:
Sets multiple keys and values from a mapping.

:param mapping: a mapping with the keys/values to set.
:param timeout: the cache timeout for the key in seconds (if not
                specified, it uses the default timeout). A timeout of
                0 indicates that the cache never expires.
:returns: A list containing all keys sucessfuly set
:rtype: boolean

methods.name: cachelib.base.BaseCache.delete_many
documentation:
Deletes multiple keys at once.

:param keys: The function accepts multiple keys as positional
             arguments.
:returns: A list containing all sucessfuly deleted keys
:rtype: boolean

methods.name: cachelib.base.BaseCache.has
documentation:
Checks if a key exists in the cache without returning it. This is a
cheap operation that bypasses loading the actual data on the backend.

:param key: the key to check

methods.name: cachelib.base.BaseCache.clear
documentation:
Clears the cache.  Keep in mind that not all caches support
completely clearing the cache.

:returns: Whether the cache has been cleared.
:rtype: boolean

methods.name: cachelib.base.BaseCache.inc
documentation:
Increments the value of a key by `delta`.  If the key does
not yet exist it is initialized with `delta`.

For supporting caches this is an atomic operation.

```{eval-rst}

:param key: the key to increment.
:param delta: the delta to add.
:returns: The new value or ``None`` for backend errors.
```

methods.name: cachelib.base.BaseCache.dec
documentation:
Decrements the value of a key by `delta`.  If the key does
not yet exist it is initialized with `-delta`.

For supporting caches this is an atomic operation.

:param key: the key to increment.
:param delta: the delta to subtract.
:returns: The new value or `None` for backend errors.

Baseclass for the cache systems.  All the cache systems implement this
API or a superset of it.

```{eval-rst}

of 0 indicates that the cache never expires.
```

classes.name: cachelib.base.NullCache
documentation:
A cache that doesn't cache.  This can be useful for unit testing.

:param default_timeout: a dummy parameter that is ignored but exists
                        for API compatibility with other caches.

classes.name: cachelib.memcached.MemcachedCache

methods.name: cachelib.memcached.MemcachedCache.import_preferred_memcache_lib
documentation: Returns an initialized memcache client.  Used by the constructor.

A cache that uses memcached as backend.

The first argument can either be an object that resembles the API of a
{class}`memcache.Client` or a tuple/list of server addresses. In the
event that a tuple/list is passed, Werkzeug tries to import the best
available memcache library.

This cache looks into the following packages/modules to find bindings for
memcached:

> - `pylibmc`
> - `google.appengine.api.memcached`
> - `memcached`
> - `libmc`

Implementation notes:  This cache backend works around some limitations in
memcached to simplify the interface.  For example unicode keys are encoded
to utf-8 on the fly.  Methods such as {meth}`~BaseCache.get_dict` return
the keys in the same format as passed.  Furthermore all get methods
silently ignore key errors to not cause problems when untrusted user data
is passed to the get methods which is often the case in web applications.

```{eval-rst}

a :class:`memcache.Client` or a compatible client.
:param default_timeout: the default timeout that is used if no timeout is
                        specified on :meth:`~BaseCache.set`. A timeout of
                        0 indicates that the cache never expires.
:param key_prefix: a prefix that is added before all keys.  This makes it
                   possible to use the same memcached server for different
                   applications.  Keep in mind that
                   :meth:`~BaseCache.clear` will also clear keys with a
                   different prefix.
```

classes.name: cachelib.file.FileSystemCache

methods.name: cachelib.file.FileSystemCache._list_dir
documentation: return a list of (fully qualified) cache filenames

methods.name: cachelib.file.FileSystemCache._run_safely
documentation:
On Windows os.replace, os.chmod and open can yield
permission errors if executed by two different processes.

A cache that stores the items on the file system.  This cache depends
on being the only user of the `cache_dir`.  Make absolutely sure that
nobody but this cache stores files there or otherwise the cache will
randomly delete files therein.

```{eval-rst}

:param cache_dir: the directory where cache files are stored.
:param threshold: the maximum number of items the cache stores before
                  it starts deleting some. A threshold value of 0
                  indicates no threshold.
:param default_timeout: the default timeout that is used if no timeout is
                        specified on :meth:`~BaseCache.set`. A timeout of
                        0 indicates that the cache never expires.
:param mode: the file mode wanted for the cache files, default 0600
:param hash_method: Default hashlib.md5. The hash method used to
                    generate the filename for cached results.
```
